query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "citevqa: benchmarking evidence attribution for trustworthy document in" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on semantic segmentation in computer vision and proposes a benchmark for visual attribution faithfulness, which is a distinct construct and domain from the user's question regarding evidence attribution in document-based Question Answering (VQA) and linguistics. It fails the "off-domain entirely" rejection rule as it addresses a different modality (pixels vs. text) and task (segmentation vs. VQA) with no measurable connection to the specific mechanism or population of the user'
